title: The contribution of 14C AMS dating to the Greater Angkor archaeological project

contribution of14C AMS dating to the Greater Angkor archaeological project  ...    For well over five centuries, the Khmer kingdom ruled over a vast territory, including most of what is now known as Cambodia, Thailand, Vietnam and Laos. Between the 9th and 14th century AD, the Khmer Kings developed the Angkor area into a remarkable administrative and religious centre for their society. ## Methods All samples discussed here were radiocarbon dated at the ANTARES AMS facility [2]. Before chemical pre-treatment, wood was milled to small particles (0.5–1.0 mm) and charcoal fragments were separated from sediment by dry sieving. The AAA method [3] was then employed to remove contamination.
